name: Butterfly Shrimp with Snowpeas
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb Fresh or frozen medium-sized shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak snowpeas in cold water for 2 hours to make crisp.
2. Soak shrimp in salted cold water for 1 hour. Drain straw mushrooms.
3. Break off ends of snowpeas. Peel and rinse water chestnuts.
4. Shell shrimp, keeping the tail intact.
Cooking:
5. Deeply slit shrimp around the upper curve (don't cut through), devein, and spread them almost flat.
6. Cut green onions on the bias into 2-inch lengths.
7. Slice water chestnuts thinly crosswise.
8. In a small bowl, mix chicken stock, soy sauce, sherry, salt, and sugar.
9. Stir-frying:
- Swirl peanut oil into a very hot wok.
- When the oil begins to smoke, add shrimp and stir-fry until they curl (about 20 seconds).
- Remove shrimp to a serving platter.
- Stir-fry mushrooms for 30 seconds; add garlic and ginger; stir-fry another 30 seconds.
- Add snowpeas and water chestnuts; stir-fry briskly for 1 minute.
- Add the stock mixture; bring to boil. Keep tossing until snowpeas are bright green.
10. Push ingredients out of the liquid, dribble in cornstarch paste to thicken slightly.
11. Return ingredients, including shrimp, to the wok. Stir briefly.
12. Serve immediately.
Note: Snowpeas should be slightly undercooked when served.
